submission.title: Cathedral Sainte-Marie-Majeur in Marseille, 1:1 reproduction, by weelly×Sekure Suwaroteiru#1000 and Babyoda#3477.

comments.body:
The Sainte-Marie-Majeure cathedral, known as La Major, is the Catholic cathedral of the Archdiocese of Marseille. La Major was built in neo-Byzantine style between 1852 and 1893 to plans by the architect Léon Vaudoyer. Located in the district of Joliette in the 2nd arrondissement, it stands on an esplanade between the Old Port and the port of Joliette, on the site of the old 12th century cathedral, from which the Occitan name comes. of "Major". Its architecture and interior decoration, in marble and porphyry, give it a particular aspect for a religious building.It was erected as a minor basilica by Leo XIII on January 24, 1896.

Builders : Sekure Suwaroteiru#1000 et Babyoda#3477



We are open to new builders, you can join by going to our server in discord ---> https://discord.gg/btefrance
this is incredible
Jesus christ
Built diagonally? You madlad
It’s a 1:1 reproduction, but also with a real orientation and elevation
Awesome work. Congrats. Diagonal builds are very hard, but you with did a great job with it.
Is this for the Build the Earth project?
yes, you can see it by the username of the OP
Oh My God that is so magnificent!  I hate you for the beauty and awesomeness of it all.
It takes a true masochist to build something like this at an angle
Thanks !
The angle is not a problem, it's even better to do details
Yes I suppose he would be

La Major was built in neo-Byzantine style between 1852 and 1893 to plans by the architect Léon Vaudoyer. :o
Le serveur BTE France possède un mod spécial pour avoir une hauteur quasi illimitée 😉
Faut l’inviter dîner mercredi soir.

Je vais poser une question idiote mais comment on reproduit à l'échelle 1:1 dans un jeu vidéo ? On considère que la hauteur du personnage est équivalente à la hauteur d'un humain réel et on part de là ?
Combien ~~d'allumettes~~ de blocs ? Allez, à votre avis ? Dites un chiffre
Il me semble avoir lu qu'un bloc fait un mètre.
Ces fdps d’hommes de l’ender sont donc très grands
C’est le projet build the earth ?
Sacrés Néerlandais
Exactement !
Effectivement 1 bloc = 1 mètre
Exactement, 1 bloc = 1 mètre.

submission.title: L'église de Saint-Nizier reproduit dans Minecraft

comments.body: Incroyable!
Love these builds in scale with debug stick detailisation
This is inhuman
Me too, We have a bunch of builder who are really good at that in BTE France !
My brain hurts from looking at this
It's good then 😈
Yea hard to tell whats real and isnt
I'm glad the illusion worked out this well
Incroyable! Even the diagonals are crazy good!!
Second pic had me TRIPPING
XD
I want to play this world so bad just so I can run around the streets in pic 2
Thought it was real... Can I steal your skill?
Alright sir, it will be 99999$
You can join BuildTheEarth France Minecraft server if you want to visit this church or anything else that has been built !
How bout tree fitty?

That's fine for me 🤝

Jokes on you, I'm not the builder of this one !
TUT scammed
Just wow.
thought for a split second that I was looking at photographs
This looks like a pixelated google earth street view, dang😭
this is so well made that i, out of nowhere, just remembered completely what it looked like when i walked past IRL some years ago
Incredible ! That is exactly the goal of our Minecraft server, glad that it got you this feeling

Wait, what? 

HOLY HELL, SOME OF ITS REAL.
That was the intended reaction 😁

That is the goal ! XD

However... Maybe I should have mentioned in the title that some of it is google earth lol
This would be impressive enough as a straight edged build, but the fact that it’s been done on a slight angle makes it really stand out!

Yes ! Because we build everything in one map the size of the earth with pregenerated outline for our builds, so they are often not straight in order to match the reality 

It's always first thought as a constraint but at one point it become a good thing because it allows for more details
:o
My toxic trait is thinking I can build this
Damn, that blends perfectly with the real images. Good job.
Debug stick can make some incredible details, but I'm not a big fan of it. Feels like cheating to me lmao. I mostly build my stuff in creative to then litematica into my survival.
Ah yes virtuel Lego. 🤣 (Don't take it the wrong way I like Minecraft)

J'adore l'incrustation avec les vrai images de Lyon !

Par contre, Pourquoi Saint-Nizier  ? C'est par raport à l'horloge ?
C'est simplement que c'est ce qui est tombé sur l'agenda hier. On a des milliers de builds sur notre serveur et le but est de poster les meilleurs sur les réseaux afin de faire connaître le projet
Thanks !
Maybe you can ;)
Ah ok ! Bien joué et bonne chance pour la suite
I really like the real world integration \^\^
Glad that you appreciate it
Looking at those streetlight banners and realizing I was doing those the hard way this whole time😅 Incredible build all around!
This build is so fucking cool and creative, and detailed, and even full of life!
What's the shader?
Thought it was a Minecraft earth build, but very impressive!

The shader is blender
/s
This the Build The Earth project so we also reproduce the other thing in Minecraft
Oh, so that's why it looks so real, someone needs to make shader similar to Blender's (Even though it would probably be very resource heavy)
Looks incredible, I love builds not completely aligned with the grid!
Blows my mind
Well this is the limiting factor X) One image like this need a full GPU for several minutes, imagine generating it 60 times every second
One day, one day...
Saint-Nizier Church looks epic, but can it host a sport game? Probably not, but still cool vibes!
